title: EVMbench: Evaluating AI Agents on Smart Contract Security
authors: Justin Wang, Andreas Bigger, Xiaohai Xu, Justin W. Lin, Andy Applebaum, Tejal Patwardhan, Alpin Yukseloglu, Olivia Watkins
abstract: Smart contracts on public blockchains now manage large amounts of value, and vulnerabilities in these systems can lead to substantial losses. As AI agents become more capable at reading, writing, and running code, it is natural to ask how well they can already navigate this landscape, both in ways that improve security and in ways that might increase risk. We introduce EVMbench, an evaluation that measures the ability of agents to detect, patch, and exploit smart contract vulnerabilities. EVMbench draws on 117 curated vulnerabilities from 40 repositories and, in the most realistic setting, uses programmatic grading based on tests and blockchain state under a local Ethereum execution environment. We evaluate a range of frontier agents and find that they are capable of discovering and exploiting vulnerabilities end-to-end against live blockchain instances. We release code, tasks, and tooling to support continued measurement of these capabilities and future work on security.
title_zh: EVMbench：在智能合约安全上评估AI代理
abstract_zh: 如今，公共区块链上的智能合约管理着巨额价值，这些系统中的漏洞可能导致重大损失。随着AI代理在阅读、编写和执行代码方面的能力不断增强，人们自然会思考：它们在当前究竟能在多大程度上应对这一领域，既包括提升安全性的能力，也包括可能带来风险的潜力。我们提出了EVMbench，这是一个评估AI代理在检测、修复和利用智能合约漏洞方面能力的基准测试。EVMbench基于来自40个代码仓库的117个精心筛选的漏洞，并在最贴近实际的环境中，采用基于测试用例和本地以太坊执行环境下的区块链状态进行程序化评分。我们对一系列前沿AI代理进行了评估，发现它们能够端到端地发现并利用漏洞，针对实时区块链实例发起攻击。我们已公开相关代码、任务和工具，以支持对这些能力的持续评估以及未来安全研究工作的开展。

title: EvoTool: Self-Evolving Tool-Use Policy Optimization in LLM Agents via Blame-Aware Mutation and Diversity-Aware Selection
authors: Shuo Yang, Soyeon Caren Han, Xueqi Ma, Yan Li, Mohammad Reza Ghasemi Madani, Eduard Hovy
abstract: LLM-based agents depend on effective tool-use policies to solve complex tasks, yet optimizing these policies remains challenging due to delayed supervision and the difficulty of credit assignment in long-horizon trajectories. Existing optimization approaches tend to be either monolithic, which are prone to entangling behaviors, or single-aspect, which ignore cross-module error propagation. To address these limitations, we propose EvoTool, a self-evolving framework that optimizes a modular tool-use policy via a gradient-free evolutionary paradigm. EvoTool decomposes agent's tool-use policy into four modules, including Planner, Selector, Caller, and Synthesizer, and iteratively improves them in a self-improving loop through three novel mechanisms. Trajectory-Grounded Blame Attribution uses diagnostic traces to localize failures to a specific module. Feedback-Guided Targeted Mutation then edits only that module via natural-language critique. Diversity-Aware Population Selection preserves complementary candidates to ensure solution diversity. Across four benchmarks, EvoTool outperforms strong baselines by over 5 points on both GPT-4.1 and Qwen3-8B, while achieving superior efficiency and transferability. The code will be released once paper is accepted.
title_zh: EvoTool：通过问责感知突变与多样性感知选择实现大语言模型智能体的自进化工具使用策略优化
abstract_zh: 基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体依赖于高效的工具使用策略来完成复杂任务，然而由于长期轨迹中存在延迟反馈以及信用分配困难，优化这些策略仍面临挑战。现有的优化方法通常要么采用整体式架构，容易导致行为纠缠；要么仅关注单一维度，忽视了模块间的错误传播问题。为解决上述局限，我们提出EvoTool——一种自进化框架，通过无梯度的进化范式优化模块化工具使用策略。EvoTool将智能体的工具使用策略分解为四个模块：规划器（Planner）、选择器（Selector）、调用器（Caller）和合成器（Synthesizer），并通过三种新颖机制在自增强循环中迭代优化这些模块。轨迹锚定的归因分析（Trajectory-Grounded Blame Attribution）利用诊断性追踪定位失败的具体模块；基于反馈的定向突变（Feedback-Guided Targeted Mutation）则通过自然语言批评仅对故障模块进行编辑；多样性感知种群选择（Diversity-Aware Population Selection）则保留互补的候选方案，以保障解空间的多样性。在四个基准测试中，EvoTool在GPT-4.1和Qwen3-8B上均显著优于强基线模型，性能提升超过5个百分点，同时展现出更优的效率与迁移能力。代码将在论文被接收后公开发布。

title: FireBench: Evaluating Instruction Following in Enterprise and API-Driven LLM Applications
authors: Yunfan Zhang, Yijie Bei, Jetashree Ravi, Pawel Garbacki
abstract: Instruction following is critical for LLMs deployed in enterprise and API-driven settings, where strict adherence to output formats, content constraints, and procedural requirements is essential for enabling reliable LLM-assisted workflows. However, existing instruction following benchmarks predominantly evaluate natural language generation constraints that reflect the needs of chat assistants rather than enterprise users. To bridge this gap, we introduce FireBench, an LLM instruction following benchmark grounded in real-world enterprise and API usage patterns. FireBench evaluates six core capability dimensions across diverse applications including information extraction, customer support, and coding agents, comprising over 2,400 samples. We evaluate 11 LLMs and present key findings on their instruction following behavior in enterprise scenarios. We open-source FireBench at fire-bench.com to help users assess model suitability, support model developers in diagnosing performance, and invite community contributions.
title_zh: FireBench：评估企业及API驱动型大语言模型应用中的指令遵循能力
abstract_zh: 指令遵循对于在企业及基于API的应用场景中部署的大语言模型（LLM）至关重要，因为在这些场景中，严格遵守输出格式、内容限制和流程要求是实现可靠LLM辅助工作流的关键。然而，现有的指令遵循评估基准主要侧重于反映聊天助手需求的自然语言生成约束，而非企业用户的真实需求。为弥合这一差距，我们推出了FireBench——一个基于真实企业及API使用模式的LLM指令遵循评估基准。FireBench在信息提取、客户支持和编码代理等多样化应用场景中，评估了六项核心能力维度，涵盖超过2,400个样本。我们对11个LLM进行了评估，并揭示了它们在企业场景下指令遵循行为的关键发现。我们已将FireBench开源，访问地址为fire-bench.com，旨在帮助用户评估模型适用性，支持模型开发者进行性能诊断，并欢迎社区参与贡献。

title: Stacked from One: Multi-Scale Self-Injection for Context Window Extension
authors: Wei Han, Pan Zhou, Shuicheng Yan
abstract: The limited context window of contemporary large language models (LLMs) remains a primary bottleneck for their broader application across diverse domains. Although continual pre-training on long-context data offers a straightforward solution, it incurs prohibitive data acquisition and computational costs. To address this challenge, we propose~\modelname, a novel framework based on multi-grained context compression and query-aware information acquisition. SharedLLM comprises two stacked short-context LLMs: a lower model serving as a compressor and an upper model acting as a decoder. The lower model compresses long inputs into compact, multi-grained representations, which are then forwarded to the upper model for context-aware processing. To maximize efficiency, this information transfer occurs exclusively at the lowest layers, bypassing lengthy forward passes and redundant cross-attention operations. This entire process, wherein the upper and lower models are derived from the same underlying LLM layers, is termed~\textit{self-injection}. To support this architecture, a specialized tree-based data structure enables the efficient encoding and query-aware retrieval of contextual information. Despite being trained on sequences of only 8K tokens, \modelname~effectively generalizes to inputs exceeding 128K tokens. Across a comprehensive suite of long-context modeling and understanding benchmarks, \modelname~achieves performance superior or comparable to strong baselines, striking an optimal balance between efficiency and accuracy. Furthermore, these design choices allow \modelname~to substantially reduce the memory footprint and yield notable inference speedups ($2\times$ over streaming and $3\times$ over encoder-decoder architectures).
title_zh: 从一而起：用于上下文窗口扩展的多尺度自注入
abstract_zh: 当代大型语言模型（LLMs）的有限上下文窗口仍是其在多样化领域广泛应用的主要瓶颈。尽管通过在长上下文数据上持续预训练可提供一种直接的解决方案，但该方法带来了高昂的数据获取与计算成本。为应对这一挑战，我们提出~\modelname，一种基于多粒度上下文压缩与查询感知信息获取的新框架。SharedLLM由两个堆叠的短上下文LLM组成：下层模型作为压缩器，上层模型作为解码器。下层模型将长输入压缩为紧凑且具有多粒度特性的表示，随后将其传递给上层模型进行上下文感知处理。为最大化效率，这种信息传递仅发生在最低层，从而跳过冗长的前向传播过程和重复的交叉注意力操作。整个过程中，上层与下层模型均源自同一底层LLM层，这一机制被称为~\textit{自注入}（self-injection）。为支持该架构，我们设计了一种专用的树形数据结构，能够高效编码并实现查询感知的信息检索。尽管训练时仅使用8K标记序列，\modelname~仍能有效泛化至超过128K标记的输入。在一系列全面的长上下文建模与理解基准测试中，\modelname~的表现优于或媲美强基线模型，在效率与准确性之间取得了最佳平衡。此外，这些设计选择使\modelname~显著降低了内存占用，并实现了显著的推理加速——相较于流式处理方案快2倍，相较于编码器-解码器架构快3倍。

title: MOOSEnger -- a Domain-Specific AI Agent for the MOOSE Ecosystem
authors: Mengnan Li, Jason Miller, Zachary Prince, Alexander Lindsay, Cody Permann
abstract: MOOSEnger is a tool-enabled AI agent tailored to the Multiphysics Object-Oriented Simulation Environment (MOOSE). MOOSE cases are specified in HIT ".i" input files; the large object catalog and strict syntax make initial setup and debugging slow. MOOSEnger offers a conversational workflow that turns natural-language intent into runnable inputs by combining retrieval-augmented generation over curated docs/examples with deterministic, MOOSE-aware parsing, validation, and execution tools. A core-plus-domain architecture separates reusable agent infrastructure (configuration, registries, tool dispatch, retrieval services, persistence, and evaluation) from a MOOSE plugin that adds HIT-based parsing, syntax-preserving ingestion of input files, and domain-specific utilities for input repair and checking. An input precheck pipeline removes hidden formatting artifacts, fixes malformed HIT structure with a bounded grammar-constrained loop, and resolves invalid object types via similarity search over an application syntax registry. Inputs are then validated and optionally smoke-tested with the MOOSE runtime in the loop via an MCP-backed execution backend (with local fallback), translating solver diagnostics into iterative verify-and-correct updates. Built-in evaluation reports RAG metrics (faithfulness, relevancy, context precision/recall) and end-to-end success by actual execution. On a 125-prompt benchmark spanning diffusion, transient heat conduction, solid mechanics, porous flow, and incompressible Navier--Stokes, MOOSEnger achieves a 0.93 execution pass rate versus 0.08 for an LLM-only baseline.
title_zh: MOOSEnger——面向MOOSE生态系统的领域专用AI代理
abstract_zh:
MOOSEnger 是一个专为多物理场面向对象仿真环境（MOOSE）定制的工具增强型AI代理。MOOSE 的仿真案例通过 HIT 格式的“.i”输入文件进行定义；由于其庞大的对象库和严格的语法要求，初始设置与调试过程往往耗时较长。MOOSEnger 提供了一种对话式工作流，通过结合经过筛选的文档与示例的检索增强生成（RAG）技术，以及确定性的、具备 MOOSE 知识的解析、验证与执行工具，将自然语言意图转化为可运行的输入文件。

该系统采用“核心+领域”架构，将可复用的代理基础设施（包括配置管理、注册表、工具调度、检索服务、持久化存储及评估机制）与一个专门针对 MOOSE 的插件分离。该插件实现了基于 HIT 的解析、保持语法结构的输入文件摄取，以及用于输入修复与检查的领域专用工具。

在输入处理阶段，系统首先通过一个预检查流水线清除隐藏的格式化干扰，利用受有限语法约束的循环修复结构错误，并通过在应用语法注册表中进行相似性搜索来解决无效对象类型问题。随后，输入文件在 MOOSE 运行时环境中通过基于 MCP 的执行后端（支持本地回退）进行验证和可选的“烟雾测试”，将求解器诊断信息转化为迭代式的验证与修正更新。

系统内置评估报告可提供 RAG 相关指标（如忠实度、相关性、上下文精确率/召回率），以及基于实际执行的端到端成功率。在涵盖扩散、瞬态热传导、固体力学、多孔介质流动及不可压缩 Navier-Stokes 方程的 125 个提示的基准测试中，MOOSEnger 实现了 0.93 的执行通过率，远超仅使用大语言模型（LLM）的基线方法（仅 0.08）。

title: VizCrit: Exploring Strategies for Displaying Computational Feedback in a Visual Design Tool
authors: Mingyi Li, Mengyi Chen, Sarah Luo, Yining Cao, Haijun Xia, Maitraye Das, Steven P. Dow, Jane L. E
abstract: Visual design instructors often provide multi-modal feedback, mixing annotations with text. Prior theory emphasizes the importance of actionable feedback, where "actionability" lies on a spectrum--from surfacing relevant design concepts to suggesting concrete fixes. How might creativity tools implement annotations that support such feedback, and how does the actionability of feedback impact novices' process-related behaviors, perceptions of creativity, learning of design principles, and overall outcomes? We introduce VizCrit, a system for providing computational feedback that supports the actionability spectrum, realized through algorithmic issue detection and visual annotation generation. In a between-subjects study (N=36), novices revised a design under one of three conditions: textbook-based, awareness-centered, or solution-centered feedback. We found that solution-centered feedback led to fewer design issues and higher self-perceived creativity compared with textbook-based feedback, although expert ratings on creativity showed no significant differences. We discuss the implications for AI in Creativity Support Tools, including the potential of calibrating feedback actionability to help novices balance productivity with learning, growth, and developing design awareness.
title_zh: VizCrit：探索在视觉设计工具中展示计算反馈的策略
abstract_zh: 视觉设计教师通常采用多模态反馈方式，将注释与文字相结合。先前的理论强调了可操作性反馈的重要性，其中“可操作性”存在于一个连续谱上——从揭示相关的设计概念，到提出具体的修改建议。那么，创意工具应如何实现支持这一反馈谱系的注释功能？反馈的可操作性又如何影响初学者在设计过程中的行为、对创造力的认知、对设计原则的学习以及最终的设计成果？我们提出了VizCrit系统，该系统通过算法检测设计问题并生成可视化注释，从而支持可操作性反馈的连续谱。在一项被试间设计实验中（N=36），初学者在三种不同反馈条件下对同一设计进行修改：基于教科书的反馈、以意识为中心的反馈，以及以解决方案为中心的反馈。研究发现，与基于教科书的反馈相比，以解决方案为中心的反馈使设计问题更少，且参与者自我感知的创造力更高，尽管专家对创造力的评分未显示出显著差异。本文讨论了人工智能在创意支持工具中的意义，包括通过校准反馈的可操作性，帮助初学者在效率与学习、成长及设计意识发展之间取得平衡的潜力。

title: cuRoboV2: Dynamics-Aware Motion Generation with Depth-Fused Distance Fields for High-DoF Robots
authors: Balakumar Sundaralingam, Adithyavairavan Murali, Stan Birchfield
abstract: Effective robot autonomy requires motion generation that is safe, feasible, and reactive. Current methods are fragmented: fast planners output physically unexecutable trajectories, reactive controllers struggle with high-fidelity perception, and existing solvers fail on high-DoF systems. We present cuRoboV2, a unified framework with three key innovations: (1) B-spline trajectory optimization that enforces smoothness and torque limits; (2) a GPU-native TSDF/ESDF perception pipeline that generates dense signed distance fields covering the full workspace, unlike existing methods that only provide distances within sparsely allocated blocks, up to 10x faster and in 8x less memory than the state-of-the-art at manipulation scale, with up to 99% collision recall; and (3) scalable GPU-native whole-body computation, namely topology-aware kinematics, differentiable inverse dynamics, and map-reduce self-collision, that achieves up to 61x speedup while also extending to high-DoF humanoids (where previous GPU implementations fail). On benchmarks, cuRoboV2 achieves 99.7% success under 3kg payload (where baselines achieve only 72--77%), 99.6% collision-free IK on a 48-DoF humanoid (where prior methods fail entirely), and 89.5% retargeting constraint satisfaction (vs. 61% for PyRoki); these collision-free motions yield locomotion policies with 21% lower tracking error than PyRoki and 12x lower cross-seed variance than mink. A ground-up codebase redesign for discoverability enabled LLM coding assistants to author up to 73% of new modules, including hand-optimized CUDA kernels, demonstrating that well-structured robotics code can unlock productive human--LLM collaboration. Together, these advances provide a unified, dynamics-aware motion generation stack that scales from single-arm manipulators to full humanoids.
title_zh: cuRoboV2：面向高自由度机器人的深度融合距离场动态感知运动生成
abstract_zh: 实现高效机器人自主性需要安全、可行且具备反应能力的运动规划。当前的方法存在碎片化问题：快速规划器生成的轨迹在物理上无法执行，反应式控制器在高保真感知方面表现不佳，而现有求解器在高自由度（DoF）系统上则难以奏效。我们提出了cuRoboV2，一个统一的框架，包含三大创新：（1）基于B样条的轨迹优化方法，可强制实现轨迹平滑性并满足扭矩限制；（2）原生GPU的TSDF/ESDF感知流水线，能够生成覆盖整个工作空间的密集符号距离场，不同于现有方法仅在稀疏分配的区块内提供距离信息，其速度比当前最先进的方法快达10倍，内存占用减少8倍，且在操作尺度下碰撞召回率高达99%；（3）可扩展的原生GPU全身体运动计算，包括拓扑感知的运动学、可微分的逆动力学以及基于map-reduce的自碰撞检测，实现了最高达61倍的加速，并成功扩展至高自由度人形机器人（此前GPU实现在此类系统上均告失败）。在基准测试中，cuRoboV2在3kg载荷下实现了99.7%的成功率（基线方法仅达72–77%），在48自由度人形机器人上实现了99.6%的无碰撞逆运动学解算（此前方法完全失效），并实现了89.5%的重定向约束满足率（相比PyRoki的61%显著提升）。这些无碰撞运动生成的行走策略相比PyRoki降低了21%的跟踪误差，相比mink降低了12倍的跨种子方差。通过从零开始重构代码库以提升可发现性，使得大语言模型（LLM）编程助手能够自主编写高达73%的新模块，包括手工优化的CUDA内核，证明了结构良好的机器人代码能够极大促进人类与LLM之间的高效协作。综上所述，这些进展共同构建了一个统一、具备动力学感知能力的运动生成系统，可从单臂机械臂无缝扩展至完整人形机器人。

title: Beyond Scattered Acceptance: Fast and Coherent Inference for DLMs via Longest Stable Prefixes
authors: Pengxiang Li, Joey Tsai, Hongwei Xue, Kunyu Shi, Shilin Yan
abstract: Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) promise highly parallel text generation, yet their practical inference speed is often bottlenecked by suboptimal decoding schedulers. Standard approaches rely on 'scattered acceptance'-committing high confidence tokens at disjoint positions throughout the sequence. This approach inadvertently fractures the Key-Value (KV) cache, destroys memory locality, and forces the model into costly, repeated repairs across unstable token boundaries. To resolve this, we present the Longest Stable Prefix (LSP) scheduler, a training-free and model-agnostic inference paradigm based on monolithic prefix absorption. In each denoising step, LSP evaluates token stability via a single forward pass, dynamically identifies a contiguous left-aligned block of stable predictions, and snaps its boundary to natural linguistic or structural delimiters before an atomic commitment. This prefix-first topology yields dual benefits: systemically, it converts fragmented KV cache updates into efficient, contiguous appends; algorithmically, it preserves bidirectional lookahead over a geometrically shrinking active suffix, drastically reducing token flip rates and denoiser calls. Extensive evaluations on LLaDA-8B and Dream-7B demonstrate that LSP accelerates inference by up to 3.4x across rigorous benchmarks including mathematical reasoning, code generation, multilingual (CJK) tasks, and creative writing while matching or slightly improving output quality. By fundamentally restructuring the commitment topology, LSP bridges the gap between the theoretical parallelism of DLMs and practical hardware efficiency.
title_zh: 超越零散接受：通过最长稳定前缀实现DLMs的快速且连贯推理
abstract_zh: 扩散语言模型（DLMs）承诺实现高度并行的文本生成，但其实际推理速度常受限于次优的解码调度策略。传统方法依赖“分散接受”——在序列中不连续的位置提交高置信度的词元。这种做法无意中破坏了键值（KV）缓存的连续性，损害了内存局部性，并迫使模型在不稳定的词元边界上反复进行代价高昂的修复操作。为解决这一问题，我们提出一种名为“最长稳定前缀”（Longest Stable Prefix, LSP）的调度器，这是一种无需训练、与模型无关的推理范式，基于整体前缀吸收机制。在每个去噪步骤中，LSP 通过一次前向传播评估词元的稳定性，动态识别出一个连续且左对齐的稳定预测块，并在原子提交前将其边界对齐至自然的语言或结构分隔符。这种“前缀优先”的拓扑结构带来了双重优势：系统层面，将碎片化的 KV 缓存更新转化为高效的连续追加；算法层面，保持了对几何级数缩小的活跃后缀的双向前瞻能力，显著降低词元翻转率和去噪器调用次数。在 LLaDA-8B 和 Dream-7B 上的大量实验表明，LSP 在包括数学推理、代码生成、多语言（中日韩）任务和创意写作在内的严格基准测试中，推理速度最高提升达 3.4 倍，同时保持或略微提升了输出质量。通过从根本上重构提交拓扑结构，LSP 有效弥合了 DLM 理论上的并行性与实际硬件效率之间的鸿沟。

title: Building AI Coding Agents for the Terminal: Scaffolding, Harness, Context Engineering, and Lessons Learned
authors: Nghi D. Q. Bui
abstract: The landscape of AI coding assistance is undergoing a fundamental shift from complex IDE plugins to versatile, terminal-native agents. Operating directly where developers manage source control, execute builds, and deploy environments, CLI-based agents offer unprecedented autonomy for long-horizon development tasks. In this paper, we present OPENDEV, an open-source, command-line coding agent engineered specifically for this new paradigm. Effective autonomous assistance requires strict safety controls and highly efficient context management to prevent context bloat and reasoning degradation. OPENDEV overcomes these challenges through a compound AI system architecture with workload-specialized model routing, a dual-agent architecture separating planning from execution, lazy tool discovery, and adaptive context compaction that progressively reduces older observations. Furthermore, it employs an automated memory system to accumulate project-specific knowledge across sessions and counteracts instruction fade-out through event-driven system reminders. By enforcing explicit reasoning phases and prioritizing context efficiency, OPENDEV provides a secure, extensible foundation for terminal-first AI assistance, offering a blueprint for robust autonomous software engineering.
title_zh: 为终端构建AI编程代理：架构搭建、测试框架、上下文工程及经验总结
abstract_zh: 人工智能代码辅助领域的格局正经历一场根本性转变，从复杂的IDE插件转向多功能、原生终端的智能代理。这些代理直接在开发者管理源代码控制、执行构建和部署环境的终端中运行，为长期复杂的开发任务提供了前所未有的自主性。本文提出OPENDEV，一个专为这一新范式设计的开源命令行代码代理。实现高效自主辅助需要严格的安全部署机制和高度高效的上下文管理，以防止上下文膨胀和推理能力退化。OPENDEV通过一种复合式AI系统架构，克服了这些挑战：采用针对不同工作负载的模型路由机制，分离规划与执行的双代理架构，懒加载工具发现机制，以及自适应上下文压缩技术，逐步减少较早的观察记录。此外，它还引入了自动化记忆系统，可在多个会话间积累项目专属知识，并通过事件驱动的系统提醒机制，有效缓解指令遗忘问题。通过强制执行明确的推理阶段并优先保障上下文效率，OPENDEV为以终端为核心的AI辅助提供了一个安全、可扩展的基础，为构建稳健的自主软件工程系统提供了可借鉴的蓝图。

title: Wire Your Way: Hardware-Contextualized Guidance and In-situ Tests for Personalized Circuit Prototyping
authors: Punn Lertjaturaphat, Jungwoo Rhee, Jaewon You, Andrea Bianchi
abstract: The increasing popularity of microcontroller platforms like Arduino enables diverse end-user developers to participate in circuit prototyping. Traditionally, follow-along tutorials serve as an essential learning method for makers, and in fact, several prior toolkits leveraged this format as a way to engage new makers. However, literature and our formative study (N=12) show that makers have unique preferences regarding the construction of their circuits and idiosyncratic ways to assess and debug problems, which contrasts with the step-by-step instructional nature of tutorials and those systems leveraging this method. To address this mismatch, we present a prototyping platform that supports personalized circuit construction and debugging. Our system utilizes an augmented breadboard, which is circuit-aware and supports on-the-fly hardware reconfiguration via contextualized guidance and in-situ circuit validation through interactive tests. Through a usability study (N=12), we demonstrate how makers leverage circuit-aware guidance and debugging to support individual building patterns.
title_zh: 布线之道：面向硬件情境的个性化电路原型设计指导与现场测试
abstract_zh: Arduino等微控制器平台的日益普及，使得各类终端用户开发者能够广泛参与电路原型设计。传统上，循序渐进的教程是创客们学习的重要方式，事实上，此前已有多个工具包采用这种形式来吸引新手创客。然而，文献研究及我们的前期调研（N=12）表明，创客在构建电路时具有独特的偏好，且在问题评估与调试方面往往采用个性化的方法，这与教程类内容所强调的线性、步骤化教学方式存在明显差异。为解决这一不匹配问题，我们提出了一种支持个性化电路构建与调试的原型设计平台。该系统采用一种具备电路感知能力的增强型面包板，能够通过情境化指导实现即时硬件重构，并通过交互式测试在原位完成电路验证。通过一项可用性研究（N=12），我们展示了创客如何利用电路感知的指导与调试功能，支持其各自独特的构建模式。

title: SEA-TS: Self-Evolving Agent for Autonomous Code Generation of Time Series Forecasting Algorithms
authors: Longkun Xu, Xiaochun Zhang, Qiantu Tuo, Rui Li
abstract: Accurate time series forecasting underpins decision-making across domains, yet conventional ML development suffers from data scarcity in new deployments, poor adaptability under distribution shift, and diminishing returns from manual iteration. We propose Self-Evolving Agent for Time Series Algorithms (SEA-TS), a framework that autonomously generates, validates, and optimizes forecasting code via an iterative self-evolution loop. Our framework introduces three key innovations: (1) Metric-Advantage Monte Carlo Tree Search (MA-MCTS), which replaces fixed rewards with a normalized advantage score for discriminative search guidance; (2) Code Review with running prompt refinement, where each executed solution undergoes automated review followed by prompt updates that encode corrective patterns, preventing recurrence of similar errors; and (3) Global Steerable Reasoning, which compares each node against global best and worst solutions, enabling cross-trajectory knowledge transfer. We adopt a MAP-Elites archive for architectural diversity. On the public Solar-Energy benchmark, SEA-TS generated code achieves a 40% MAE reduction relative to TimeMixer, surpassing state-of-the-art methods. On proprietary datasets, SEA-TS generated code reduces WAPE by 8.6% on solar PV forecasting and 7.7% on residential load forecasting compared to human-engineered baselines, and achieves 26.17% MAPE on load forecasting versus 29.34% by TimeMixer. Notably, the evolved models discover novel architectural patterns--including physics-informed monotonic decay heads encoding solar irradiance constraints, per-station learned diurnal cycle profiles, and learnable hourly bias correction--demonstrating that autonomous ML engineering can generate genuinely novel algorithmic ideas beyond manual design.
title_zh: SEA-TS：用于时间序列预测算法自主代码生成的自演化智能体
abstract_zh: 精准的时间序列预测在各个领域中支撑着决策制定，然而传统的机器学习开发在新部署场景下面临数据稀缺、在分布变化下适应性差，以及人工迭代带来的收益递减等问题。我们提出了自演化时间序列算法框架（Self-Evolving Agent for Time Series Algorithms, SEA-TS），该框架通过一个迭代的自我演化循环，实现预测代码的自主生成、验证与优化。本框架引入三项关键创新：（1）度量优势蒙特卡洛树搜索（Metric-Advantage Monte Carlo Tree Search, MA-MCTS），以归一化的优势得分替代固定奖励，提供更具区分性的搜索引导；（2）带运行时提示优化的代码审查机制，每个执行的解决方案均经过自动化审查，并通过更新提示词编码纠错模式，防止同类错误重复发生；（3）全局可引导推理机制，将每个节点与全局最优与最差解进行对比，实现跨轨迹的知识迁移。我们采用MAP-Elites档案库以保障模型架构的多样性。在公开的Solar-Energy基准测试中，SEA-TS生成的代码相较TimeMixer实现了40%的MAE降低，超越了现有最先进方法。在专有数据集上，SEA-TS生成的代码在太阳能光伏发电预测中将WAPE降低8.6%，在居民用电负荷预测中降低7.7%，相较于人工设计的基线模型表现更优；在负荷预测任务中，其MAPE达到26.17%，优于TimeMixer的29.34%。值得注意的是，演化出的模型发现了全新的架构模式——包括融合物理约束的单调衰减头（编码太阳辐照度限制）、基于站点的自学习昼夜周期特征，以及可学习的小时级偏差校正机制——这表明自主机器学习工程不仅能超越人工设计，还能生成真正新颖的算法构想。

title: Hardware-Software Co-design for 3D-DRAM-based LLM Serving Accelerator
authors: Cong Li, Yihan Yin, Chenhao Xue, Zhao Wang, Fujun Bai, Yixin Guo, Xiping Jiang, Qiang Wu, Yuan Xie, Guangyu Sun
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have been widely deployed for online generative services, where numerous LLM instances jointly handle workloads with fluctuating request arrival rates and variable request lengths. To efficiently execute coexisting compute-intensive and memory-intensive operators, near-memory processing (NMP) based computing paradigm has been extensively proposed. However, existing NMP designs adopt coarse-grained KV cache management and inflexible attention execution flow. Such limitations hinder these proposals from efficiently handling \textit{highly dynamic} LLM serving workloads, limiting their ability to accelerate LLM serving. To tackle these problems, we propose Helios, a Hybrid-bonding-based \uline{L}LM \uline{S}erving accelerator. Helios aims to bridge the fundamental gap between the dynamic nature of KV cache management in LLM serving and the distributed, non-uniform memory abstraction among NMP processing engines (PEs). To this end, we design both the intra-PE execution flow and the inter-PE communication primitives for distributed tiled attention execution. We further propose \textit{spatially-aware} KV cache allocation mechanism to balance the attention workload distribution while minimizing the inter-PE data transfer overhead. Compared with existing GPU/NMP designs, Helios achieves 3.25 times (geomean) speedup and 3.36 times (geomean) better energy efficiency, along with up to 72%/76% P50/P99 time-between-tokens degradation.
title_zh: 基于3D-DRAM的大型语言模型服务加速器的软硬件协同设计
abstract_zh: 大规模语言模型（LLMs）已被广泛部署于在线生成服务中，众多 LLM 实例共同处理请求到达率波动、请求长度各异的动态工作负载。为高效执行共存的计算密集型与内存密集型操作，基于近内存计算（NMP）的计算范式被广泛提出。然而，现有 NMP 设计采用粗粒度的 KV 缓存管理机制和僵化的注意力计算流程，这些局限性阻碍了其在高度动态的 LLM 服务工作负载下的高效处理，限制了其加速 LLM 服务的能力。为解决上述问题，我们提出 Helios——一种基于混合键合（Hybrid-bonding）的 LLM 服务加速器。Helios 的目标是弥合 LLM 服务中 KV 缓存管理的动态特性与 NMP 处理引擎（PEs）之间分布式、非均匀内存抽象之间的根本性差距。为此，我们设计了 PE 内的执行流程以及 PE 间的通信原语，以支持分布式分块注意力计算。此外，我们进一步提出一种“空间感知”式 KV 缓存分配机制，在平衡注意力计算负载分布的同时，最大限度地减少 PE 间的数据传输开销。与现有 GPU/NMP 设计相比，Helios 在几何平均上实现了 3.25 倍的加速比和 3.36 倍更优的能效比，同时将 P50/P99 的 token 间延迟提升控制在 72%/76% 以内。

title: UniM: A Unified Any-to-Any Interleaved Multimodal Benchmark
authors: Yanlin Li, Minghui Guo, Kaiwen Zhang, Shize Zhang, Yiran Zhao, Haodong Li, Congyue Zhou, Weijie Zheng, Yushen Yan, Shengqiong Wu, Wei Ji, Lei Cui, Furu Wei, Hao Fei, Mong-Li Lee, Wynne Hsu
abstract: In real-world multimodal applications, systems usually need to comprehend arbitrarily combined and interleaved multimodal inputs from users, while also generating outputs in any interleaved multimedia form. This capability defines the goal of any-to-any interleaved multimodal learning under a unified paradigm of understanding and generation, posing new challenges and opportunities for advancing Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). To foster and benchmark this capability, this paper introduces the UniM benchmark, the first Unified Any-to-Any Interleaved Multimodal dataset. UniM contains 31K high-quality instances across 30 domains and 7 representative modalities: text, image, audio, video, document, code, and 3D, each requiring multiple intertwined reasoning and generation capabilities. We further introduce the UniM Evaluation Suite, which assesses models along three dimensions: Semantic Correctness & Generation Quality, Response Structure Integrity, and Interleaved Coherence. In addition, we propose UniMA, an agentic baseline model equipped with traceable reasoning for structured interleaved generation. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate the difficulty of UniM and highlight key challenges and directions for advancing unified any-to-any multimodal intelligence. The project page is https://any2any-mllm.github.io/unim.
title_zh: UniM：一个统一的任意到任意交错多模态基准
abstract_zh: 在现实世界的多模态应用中，系统通常需要理解用户任意组合且交错的多模态输入，同时以任意交错的多媒体形式生成输出。这种能力定义了统一理解与生成范式下“任意到任意”交错多模态学习的目标，为推进多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）带来了新的挑战与机遇。为了促进并评估这一能力，本文提出了UniM基准，这是首个统一的“任意到任意”交错多模态数据集。UniM包含30个领域、7种代表性模态（文本、图像、音频、视频、文档、代码和3D）的31,000个高质量样本，每项任务均需多种交织的推理与生成能力。我们进一步推出了UniM评估套件，从三个维度评估模型表现：语义正确性与生成质量、响应结构完整性，以及交错连贯性。此外，我们提出了UniMA——一种具备可追溯推理能力的代理基线模型，用于支持结构化的交错生成。全面的实验结果揭示了UniM的难度，并指出了推动统一“任意到任意”多模态智能发展的关键挑战与方向。项目主页为 https://any2any-mllm.github.io/unim。

title: A framework for assessing the capabilities of code generation of constraint domain-specific languages with large language models
authors: David Delgado, Lola Burgueño, Robert Clarisó
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) can be used to support software development tasks, e.g., through code completion or code generation. However, their effectiveness drops significantly when considering less popular programming languages such as domain-specific languages (DSLs). In this paper, we propose a generic framework for evaluating the capabilities of LLMs generating DSL code from textual specifications. The generated code is assessed from the perspectives of well-formedness and correctness. This framework is applied to a particular type of DSL, constraint languages, focusing our experiments on OCL and Alloy and comparing their results to those achieved for Python, a popular general-purpose programming language. Experimental results show that, in general, LLMs have better performance for Python than for OCL and Alloy. LLMs with smaller context windows such as open-source LLMs may be unable to generate constraint-related code, as this requires managing both the constraint and the domain model where it is defined. Moreover, some improvements to the code generation process such as code repair (asking an LLM to fix incorrect code) or multiple attempts (generating several candidates for each coding task) can improve the quality of the generated code. Meanwhile, other decisions like the choice of a prompt template have less impact. All these dimensions can be systematically analyzed using our evaluation framework, making it possible to decide the most effective way to set up code generation for a particular type of task.
title_zh: 一种用于评估大语言模型在约束领域特定语言代码生成能力的框架
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）可用于支持软件开发任务，例如代码补全或代码生成。然而，当涉及较不流行编程语言（如领域特定语言，DSL）时，其有效性会显著下降。本文提出了一种通用框架，用于评估LLMs根据文本规范生成DSL代码的能力。生成的代码从语法正确性与语义正确性两个角度进行评估。该框架被应用于一种特定类型的DSL——约束语言，实验聚焦于OCL和Alloy，并将其结果与Python（一种流行的通用编程语言）进行对比。实验结果表明，总体而言，LLMs在Python上的表现优于在OCL和Alloy上的表现。对于上下文窗口较小的模型（如开源LLMs），可能无法生成与约束相关的代码，因为这类任务需要同时处理约束本身及其所属的领域模型。此外，一些代码生成过程的改进策略，如代码修复（让LLM修正错误代码）或多轮尝试（为每个任务生成多个候选代码），能够有效提升生成代码的质量。而其他因素，如提示模板的选择，则影响较小。通过本评估框架，可以系统性地分析这些不同维度，从而确定针对特定任务最有效的代码生成配置方式。

title: Free Lunch for Pass@$k$? Low Cost Diverse Sampling for Diffusion Language Models
authors: Sean Lamont, Christian Walder, Paul Montague, Amir Dezfouli, Michael Norrish
abstract: Diverse outputs in text generation are necessary for effective exploration in complex reasoning tasks, such as code generation and mathematical problem solving. Such Pass@$k$ problems benefit from distinct candidates covering the solution space. However, traditional sampling approaches often waste computational resources on repetitive failure modes. While Diffusion Language Models have emerged as a competitive alternative to the prevailing Autoregressive paradigm, they remain susceptible to this redundancy, with independent samples frequently collapsing into similar modes. To address this, we propose a training free, low cost intervention to enhance generative diversity in Diffusion Language Models. Our approach modifies intermediate samples in a batch sequentially, where each sample is repelled from the feature space of previous samples, actively penalising redundancy. Unlike prior methods that require retraining or beam search, our strategy incurs negligible computational overhead, while ensuring that each sample contributes a unique perspective to the batch. We evaluate our method on the HumanEval and GSM8K benchmarks using the LLaDA-8B-Instruct model. Our results demonstrate significantly improved diversity and Pass@$k$ performance across various temperature settings. As a simple modification to the sampling process, our method offers an immediate, low-cost improvement for current and future Diffusion Language Models in tasks that benefit from diverse solution search. We make our code available at https://github.com/sean-lamont/odd.
title_zh: Pass@$k$ 的免费午餐？用于扩散语言模型的低成本多样化采样
abstract_zh: 在复杂推理任务（如代码生成和数学问题求解）中，文本生成的多样性对于有效探索至关重要。这类 Pass@$k$ 问题需要生成多个不同的候选解，以覆盖整个解空间。然而，传统的采样方法往往在重复的失败模式上浪费大量计算资源。尽管扩散语言模型（Diffusion Language Models）已成为当前主流自回归范式的一个有力竞争者，但它们仍然容易出现冗余问题：独立生成的样本常常坍缩到相似的模式中。为解决这一问题，我们提出了一种无需训练、成本极低的干预方法，以提升扩散语言模型的生成多样性。我们的方法在批量生成过程中，对中间样本进行顺序调整，使每个样本在特征空间中被“排斥”于先前样本，从而主动惩罚重复性。与需要重新训练或使用束搜索（beam search）的先前方法不同，我们的策略计算开销极小，同时确保每个样本为整个批次贡献独特的视角。我们在 HumanEval 和 GSM8K 基准上，使用 LLaDA-8B-Instruct 模型对所提方法进行了评估。结果表明，无论在何种温度设置下，我们的方法均显著提升了生成多样性与 Pass@$k$ 性能。作为一种对采样过程的简单修改，该方法为当前及未来的扩散语言模型在需要多样化解空间搜索的任务中，提供了即插即用、低成本的性能提升。代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/sean-lamont/odd。

title: A Benchmarking Framework for Model Datasets
authors: Philipp-Lorenz Glaser, Lola Burgueño, Dominik Bork
abstract: Empirical and LLM-based research in model-driven engineering increasingly relies on datasets of software models, for instance, to train or evaluate machine learning techniques for modeling support. These datasets have a significant impact on solution performance; hence, they should be treated and assessed as first-class artifacts. However, such datasets are typically collected or created ad hoc and without guarantees of their quality for the specific task for which they are used. This limits the comparability of results between studies, obscures dataset quality and representativeness, and leads to weak reproducibility and potential bias. In this work, we propose a benchmarking framework for model datasets (i.e., benchmarking the dataset itself). Benchmarking datasets involves systematically measuring their quality, representativeness, and suitability for specific tasks. To this end, we propose a Benchmark Platform for MDE that provides a unified infrastructure for systematically assessing and comparing datasets of software models across languages and formats, using defined criteria and metrics.
title_zh: 模型数据集的基准测试框架
abstract_zh: 在模型驱动工程（Model-Driven Engineering, MDE）领域，基于实证研究和大语言模型（LLM）的研究越来越多地依赖于软件模型数据集，例如用于训练或评估支持建模的机器学习技术。这些数据集对解决方案的性能具有显著影响，因此应将其视为第一类核心资产进行处理和评估。然而，目前这些数据集通常以临时性方式收集或创建，且缺乏针对特定任务质量的保障。这导致不同研究之间的结果难以比较，数据集的质量与代表性不清晰，进而造成可复现性较弱以及潜在的偏差。本文提出了一种针对模型数据集的基准测试框架（即对数据集本身进行基准测试）。数据集的基准测试涉及系统性地衡量其质量、代表性以及对特定任务的适用性。为此，我们提出了一个面向MDE的基准测试平台，该平台提供统一的基础设施，能够基于既定标准和度量指标，系统地评估和比较跨语言、跨格式的软件模型数据集。

title: AI+HW 2035: Shaping the Next Decade
authors: Deming Chen, Jason Cong, Azalia Mirhoseini, Christos Kozyrakis, Subhasish Mitra, Jinjun Xiong, Cliff Young, Anima Anandkumar, Michael Littman, Aron Kirschen, Sophia Shao, Serge Leef, Naresh Shanbhag, Dejan Milojicic, Michael Schulte, Gert Cauwenberghs, Jerry M. Chow, Tri Dao, Kailash Gopalakrishnan, Richard Ho, Hoshik Kim, Kunle Olukotun, David Z. Pan, Mark Ren, Dan Roth, Aarti Singh, Yizhou Sun, Yusu Wang, Yann LeCun, Ruchir Puri
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) and hardware (HW) are advancing at unprecedented rates, yet their trajectories have become inseparably intertwined. The global research community lacks a cohesive, long-term vision to strategically coordinate the development of AI and HW. This fragmentation constrains progress toward holistic, sustainable, and adaptive AI systems capable of learning, reasoning, and operating efficiently across cloud, edge, and physical environments. The future of AI depends not only on scaling intelligence, but on scaling efficiency, achieving exponential gains in intelligence per joule, rather than unbounded compute consumption. Addressing this grand challenge requires rethinking the entire computing stack. This vision paper lays out a 10-year roadmap for AI+HW co-design and co-development, spanning algorithms, architectures, systems, and sustainability. We articulate key insights that redefine scaling around energy efficiency, system-level integration, and cross-layer optimization. We identify key challenges and opportunities, candidly assess potential obstacles and pitfalls, and propose integrated solutions grounded in algorithmic innovation, hardware advances, and software abstraction. Looking ahead, we define what success means in 10 years: achieving a 1000x improvement in efficiency for AI training and inference; enabling energy-aware, self-optimizing systems that seamlessly span cloud, edge, and physical AI; democratizing access to advanced AI infrastructure; and embedding human-centric principles into the design of intelligent systems. Finally, we outline concrete action items for academia, industry, government, and the broader community, calling for coordinated national initiatives, shared infrastructure, workforce development, cross-agency collaboration, and sustained public-private partnerships to ensure that AI+HW co-design becomes a unifying long-term mission.
title_zh: AI+硬件 2035：塑造下一个十年
abstract_zh: 人工智能（AI）与硬件（HW）正以前所未有的速度发展，但二者的发展轨迹已密不可分地交织在一起。全球科研界缺乏一个连贯且具有长远视野的战略框架，以系统性协调AI与硬件的协同发展。这种碎片化局面严重制约了迈向整体性、可持续且具备自适应能力的AI系统的进步，而这些系统需具备学习、推理和在云、边缘及物理环境间高效运行的能力。AI的未来不仅取决于智能规模的扩展，更在于效率的提升——实现每焦耳能量下智能性能的指数级增长，而非无限制地消耗计算资源。应对这一重大挑战，需要重新思考整个计算体系结构。本文提出一份为期十年的AI+硬件协同设计与开发路线图，涵盖算法、架构、系统及可持续性等多个层面。我们阐明了若干关键洞见，重新定义“规模化”的核心为能效、系统级集成以及跨层级优化。我们识别出关键挑战与机遇，坦诚评估潜在障碍与陷阱，并提出基于算法创新、硬件突破与软件抽象的综合解决方案。展望未来，我们明确了十年后的成功标准：实现AI训练与推理效率提升1000倍；构建能够感知能耗、自我优化并无缝贯通云、边与物理世界的智能系统；推动先进AI基础设施的普惠化；并将以人为本的原则深度融入智能系统的设计之中。最后，我们列出了学术界、产业界、政府及更广泛社区应采取的具体行动建议，呼吁启动协调一致的国家级计划，共建共享基础设施，加强人才队伍建设，推动跨部门协作，并维持持续的公私合作，确保AI+硬件协同设计成为一项凝聚共识的长期使命。

title: NL2GDS: LLM-aided interface for Open Source Chip Design
authors: Max Eland, Jeyan Thiyagalingam, Dinesh Pamunuwa, Roshan Weerasekera
abstract: The growing complexity of hardware design and the widening gap between high-level specifications and register-transfer level (RTL) implementation hinder rapid prototyping and system design. We introduce NL2GDS (Natural Language to Layout), a novel framework that leverages large language models (LLMs) to translate natural language hardware descriptions into synthesizable RTL and complete GDSII layouts via the open-source OpenLane ASIC flow. NL2GDS employs a modular pipeline that captures informal design intent, generates HDL using multiple LLM engines and verifies them, and orchestrates automated synthesis and layout. Evaluations on ISCAS'85 and ISCAS'89 benchmark designs demonstrate up to 36% area reduction, 35% delay reduction, and 70% power savings compared to baseline designs, highlighting its potential to democratize ASIC design and accelerate hardware innovation.
title_zh: NL2GDS：基于大语言模型的开源芯片设计接口
abstract_zh: 硬件设计的复杂性日益增加，而高层规格与寄存器传输级（RTL）实现之间的差距不断扩大，严重阻碍了快速原型设计和系统开发。我们提出了 NL2GDS（自然语言到版图）框架，这是一种创新方法，利用大型语言模型（LLM）将自然语言硬件描述自动转换为可综合的RTL代码以及完整的GDSII版图，整个过程基于开源的OpenLane ASIC流程。NL2GDS采用模块化流水线设计，能够捕捉非正式的设计意图，通过多个LLM引擎生成硬件描述语言（HDL）并进行验证，同时协调自动化综合与版图生成。在ISCAS'85和ISCAS'89基准设计上的评估表明，与基线设计相比，NL2GDS可实现最高达36%的面积缩减、35%的延迟降低以及70%的功耗节省，充分展示了其在推动ASIC设计民主化和加速硬件创新方面的巨大潜力。

title: Ensembling Language Models with Sequential Monte Carlo
authors: Robin Shing Moon Chan, Tianyu Liu, Samuel Kiegeland, Clemente Pasti, Jacob Hoover Vigly, Timothy J. O'Donnell, Ryan Cotterell, Tim Vieira
abstract: Practitioners have access to an abundance of language models and prompting strategies for solving many language modeling tasks; yet prior work shows that modeling performance is highly sensitive to both choices. Classical machine learning ensembling techniques offer a principled approach: aggregate predictions from multiple sources to achieve better performance than any single one. However, applying ensembling to language models during decoding is challenging: naively aggregating next-token probabilities yields samples from a locally normalized, biased approximation of the generally intractable ensemble distribution over strings. In this work, we introduce a unified framework for composing $K$ language models into $f$-ensemble distributions for a wide range of functions $f\colon\mathbb{R}_{\geq 0}^{K}\to\mathbb{R}_{\geq 0}$. To sample from these distributions, we propose a byte-level sequential Monte Carlo (SMC) algorithm that operates in a shared character space, enabling ensembles of models with mismatching vocabularies and consistent sampling in the limit. We evaluate a family of $f$-ensembles across prompt and model combinations for various structured text generation tasks, highlighting the benefits of alternative aggregation strategies over traditional probability averaging, and showing that better posterior approximations can yield better ensemble performance.
title_zh: 使用序列蒙特卡洛方法集成语言模型
abstract_zh: 实践者在解决各类语言建模任务时，可利用大量语言模型和提示策略；然而，先前的研究表明，模型性能对这些选择极为敏感。经典机器学习的集成方法提供了一种合理的方法：通过聚合多个来源的预测结果，实现优于任一单一模型的性能。然而，在解码过程中对语言模型应用集成却面临挑战：简单地对下一个词的概率进行平均，会生成一个局部归一化且有偏差的近似分布，该分布通常难以精确计算。在本研究中，我们提出一个统一框架，将 $K$ 个语言模型组合成一类广义的 $f$-集成分布，其中函数 $f\colon\mathbb{R}_{\geq 0}^{K}\to\mathbb{R}_{\geq 0}$ 覆盖多种聚合方式。为从这些分布中采样，我们提出一种基于字节级别的顺序蒙特卡洛（SMC）算法，该算法在共享字符空间中运行，能够处理词汇表不匹配的语言模型，并在极限情况下实现一致的采样。我们在多种结构化文本生成任务中，针对不同的提示与模型组合，评估了多类 $f$-集成方法，结果表明，相较于传统的概率平均策略，采用其他聚合方式具有明显优势，且更优的后验近似能够带来更好的集成性能。

title: X-RAY: Mapping LLM Reasoning Capability via Formalized and Calibrated Probes
authors: Gao Tianxi, Cai Yufan, Yuan Yusi, Dong Jin Song
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) achieve promising performance, yet their ability to reason remains poorly understood. Existing evaluations largely emphasize task-level accuracy, often conflating pattern matching with reasoning capability. We present X-RAY, an explainable reasoning analysis system that maps the LLM reasoning capability using calibrated, formally verified probes. We model reasoning capability as a function of extractable \textit{structure}, operationalized through formal properties such as constraint interaction, reasoning depth, and solution-space geometry. X-Ray generates probes via formal tools with controlled structural variations, enabling precise isolation of incremental structural information through formal calibration and verification. We evaluate state-of-the-art LLMs on problems ranging from junior-level to advanced in mathematics, physics, and chemistry. Our analysis reveals a systematic asymmetry in LLM reasoning: models are relatively robust to constraint refinement, where additional conditions shrink an existing solution space, but degrade sharply under solution-space restructuring, where modifications alter the underlying structural form of the solution manifold. Moreover, calibrated formal probes differentiate models that appear indistinguishable on standard benchmarks and reveal failure modes that are structurally interpretable rather than opaque. Beyond evaluation, our framework is contamination-free and supports the training and testing of reasoning models.
title_zh: X光：通过形式化与校准的探测器映射大语言模型的推理能力
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）取得了令人瞩目的性能表现，但其推理能力仍缺乏深入理解。现有的评估方法主要关注任务层面的准确率，常常将模式匹配与真正的推理能力混淆。我们提出了X-RAY——一种可解释的推理分析系统，通过经过校准且形式化验证的探测工具，对LLM的推理能力进行映射。我们将推理能力建模为可提取的**结构**的函数，并通过诸如约束交互、推理深度和解空间几何等形式化属性来具体实现。X-RAY利用形式化工具生成具有受控结构变化的探测问题，结合形式化校准与验证，能够精确分离出增量性的结构信息。我们在数学、物理和化学领域中从初级到高级水平的问题上评估了当前最先进的LLMs。我们的分析揭示了LLM推理中存在系统性不对称：模型在面对约束细化（即新增条件缩小已有解空间）时相对稳健，但在解空间重构（即修改改变了解流形的基本结构形式）时则急剧退化。此外，经过校准的形式化探测工具能够区分在标准基准测试中看似无法区分的模型，并揭示出具有结构可解释性的失败模式，而非模糊不清的黑箱行为。除了评估之外，该框架具备无污染特性，支持推理模型的训练与测试。

title: C2-Faith: Benchmarking LLM Judges for Causal and Coverage Faithfulness in Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
authors: Avni Mittal, Rauno Arike
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as judges of chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning, but it remains unclear whether they can reliably assess process faithfulness rather than just answer plausibility. We introduce C2-Faith, a benchmark built from PRM800K that targets two complementary dimensions of faithfulness: causality (does each step logically follow from prior context?) and coverage (are essential intermediate inferences present?). Using controlled perturbations, we create examples with known causal error positions by replacing a single step with an acausal variant, and with controlled coverage deletions at varying deletion rates (scored against reference labels). We evaluate three frontier judges under three tasks: binary causal detection, causal step localization, and coverage scoring. The results show that model rankings depend strongly on task framing, with no single judge dominating all settings; all judges exhibit a substantial gap between detecting an error and localizing it; and coverage judgments are systematically inflated for incomplete reasoning. These findings clarify when LLM judges are dependable and where they fail, and provide practical guidance for selecting judges in process-level evaluation
title_zh: C2-信念：面向思维链推理中因果与覆盖忠实性的大语言模型评判基准
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）越来越多地被用作链式思维（CoT）推理的评判者，但目前尚不清楚它们是否能够可靠地评估推理过程的忠实性，而非仅仅判断答案的合理性。为此，我们提出了C2-Faith基准，该基准基于PRM800K构建，旨在衡量忠实性的两个互补维度：因果性（每一步是否逻辑上由前序内容推导而来）和覆盖度（关键的中间推理是否完整呈现）。通过受控扰动，我们生成了具有已知因果错误位置的样本，方法是将某一步替换为非因果的变体；同时，我们以不同删除率进行受控的覆盖度删除（以参考标签为标准进行评分）。我们在三个任务中评估了三种前沿评判模型：二元因果检测、因果步骤定位和覆盖度评分。结果表明，模型的排名高度依赖于任务的表述方式，没有任何一个评判模型在所有场景中均占优；所有评判模型在识别错误与准确定位错误之间均存在显著差距；对于不完整的推理过程，覆盖度判断普遍被系统性高估。这些发现明确了LLM评判者在何种情况下可靠、在何处存在局限，并为过程级评估中评判模型的选择提供了实用指导。

title: Breaking Contextual Inertia: Reinforcement Learning with Single-Turn Anchors for Stable Multi-Turn Interaction
authors: Xingwu Chen, Zhanqiu Zhang, Yiwen Guo, Difan Zou
abstract: While LLMs demonstrate strong reasoning capabilities when provided with full information in a single turn, they exhibit substantial vulnerability in multi-turn interactions. Specifically, when information is revealed incrementally or requires updates, models frequently fail to integrate new constraints, leading to a collapse in performance compared to their single-turn baselines. We term the root cause as \emph{Contextual Inertia}: a phenomenon where models rigidly adhere to previous reasoning traces. Even when users explicitly provide corrections or new data in later turns, the model ignores them, preferring to maintain consistency with its previous (incorrect) reasoning path. To address this, we introduce \textbf{R}einforcement \textbf{L}earning with \textbf{S}ingle-\textbf{T}urn \textbf{A}nchors (\textbf{RLSTA}), a generalizable training approach designed to stabilize multi-turn interaction across diverse scenarios and domains. RLSTA leverages the model's superior single-turn capabilities as stable internal anchors to provide reward signals. By aligning multi-turn responses with these anchors, RLSTA empowers models to break contextual inertia and self-calibrate their reasoning based on the latest information. Experiments show that RLSTA significantly outperforms standard fine-tuning and abstention-based methods. Notably, our method exhibits strong cross-domain generalization (e.g., math to code) and proves effective even without external verifiers, highlighting its potential for general-domain applications.
title_zh: 打破上下文惯性：基于单轮锚点的强化学习实现稳定多轮交互
abstract_zh: 尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）在单轮交互中能够基于完整信息展现出强大的推理能力，但在多轮交互中却表现出显著的脆弱性。具体而言，当信息逐步披露或需要更新时，模型往往无法有效整合新的约束条件，导致其性能大幅下降，远低于单轮情况下的表现。我们称这一根本原因为**情境惯性**（Contextual Inertia）：即模型固执地沿袭先前的推理路径，难以调整。即使用户在后续轮次中明确提供纠正信息或新数据，模型仍倾向于维持其先前（错误的）推理轨迹，而忽略新输入。为解决这一问题，我们提出一种可泛化的训练方法——**基于单轮锚点的强化学习**（Reinforcement Learning with Single-Turn Anchors, RLSTA）。该方法利用模型在单轮任务中卓越的表现作为稳定内部锚点，生成奖励信号。通过将多轮响应与这些锚点对齐，RLSTA能够帮助模型打破情境惯性，根据最新信息自主校准推理过程。实验表明，RLSTA显著优于标准微调和基于回避策略的方法。尤为突出的是，该方法展现出强大的跨领域泛化能力（如从数学推理到代码生成），且无需依赖外部验证器即可有效工作，凸显其在通用领域应用中的巨大潜力。

title: Evaluating the Search Agent in a Parallel World
authors: Jiawei Chen, Xintian Shen, Lihao Zheng, Lifu Mu, Haoyi Sun, Ning Mao, Hao Ma, Tao Wei, Pan Zhou, Kun Zhan
abstract: Integrating web search tools has significantly extended the capability of LLMs to address open-world, real-time, and long-tail problems. However, evaluating these Search Agents presents formidable challenges. First, constructing high-quality deep search benchmarks is prohibitively expensive, while unverified synthetic data often suffers from unreliable sources. Second, static benchmarks face dynamic obsolescence: as internet information evolves, complex queries requiring deep research often degrade into simple retrieval tasks due to increased popularity, and ground truths become outdated due to temporal shifts. Third, attribution ambiguity confounds evaluation, as an agent's performance is often dominated by its parametric memory rather than its actual search and reasoning capabilities. Finally, reliance on specific commercial search engines introduces variability that hampers reproducibility. To address these issues, we propose a novel framework, Mind-ParaWorld, for evaluating Search Agents in a Parallel World. Specifically, MPW samples real-world entity names to synthesize future scenarios and questions situated beyond the model's knowledge cutoff. A ParaWorld Law Model then constructs a set of indivisible Atomic Facts and a unique ground-truth for each question. During evaluation, instead of retrieving real-world results, the agent interacts with a ParaWorld Engine Model that dynamically generates SERPs grounded in these inviolable Atomic Facts. We release MPW-Bench, an interactive benchmark spanning 19 domains with 1,608 instances. Experiments across three evaluation settings show that, while search agents are strong at evidence synthesis given complete information, their performance is limited not only by evidence collection and coverage in unfamiliar search environments, but also by unreliable evidence sufficiency judgment and when-to-stop decisions-bottlenecks.
title_zh: 在平行世界中评估搜索代理
abstract_zh:
集成网络搜索工具显著扩展了大语言模型（LLM）在开放世界、实时性以及长尾问题上的解决能力。然而，对这些搜索智能体（Search Agents）的评估仍面临巨大挑战。首先，构建高质量的深度搜索基准数据集成本极高，而未经验证的合成数据往往存在不可靠的信息来源问题。其次，静态基准存在动态过时的问题：随着互联网信息的不断演变，原本需要深入研究的复杂查询因关注度上升，逐渐退化为简单的信息检索任务，同时其真实答案也因时间推移而变得过时。第三，归因模糊性干扰了评估的准确性——智能体的表现常常更多依赖于其参数化记忆，而非真实的搜索与推理能力。最后，对特定商业搜索引擎的依赖引入了变量，影响了实验结果的可复现性。

为应对上述挑战，我们提出一种名为 Mind-ParaWorld（MPW）的新框架，用于在“平行世界”中评估搜索智能体。具体而言，MPW 通过采样现实世界中的实体名称，生成超越模型知识截止时间的未来情景和问题。随后，一个“平行世界法则模型”（ParaWorld Law Model）会构建一组不可分割的原子事实（Atomic Facts），并为每个问题生成唯一的真实答案。在评估过程中，智能体并非访问真实世界的结果，而是与一个“平行世界引擎模型”（ParaWorld Engine Model）交互，该引擎基于这些不可动摇的原子事实动态生成搜索结果页面（SERPs）。

我们发布了 MPW-Bench，这是一个涵盖19个领域的交互式基准测试集，包含1,608个实例。在三种不同评估设置下的实验表明：尽管当提供完整信息时，搜索智能体在证据整合方面表现出色，但其性能受限于多个关键瓶颈——包括在陌生搜索环境中证据的收集与覆盖能力不足，对证据充分性的判断不可靠，以及何时停止搜索的决策能力薄弱。

title: Distilling Formal Logic into Neural Spaces: A Kernel Alignment Approach for Signal Temporal Logic
authors: Sara Candussio, Gabriele Sarti, Gaia Saveri, Luca Bortolussi
abstract: We introduce a framework for learning continuous neural representations of formal specifications by distilling the geometry of their semantics into a latent space. Existing approaches rely either on symbolic kernels -- which preserve behavioural semantics but are computationally prohibitive, anchor-dependent, and non-invertible -- or on syntax-based neural embeddings that fail to capture underlying structures. Our method bridges this gap: using a teacher-student setup, we distill a symbolic robustness kernel into a Transformer encoder. Unlike standard contrastive methods, we supervise the model with a continuous, kernel-weighted geometric alignment objective that penalizes errors in proportion to their semantic discrepancies. Once trained, the encoder produces embeddings in a single forward pass, effectively mimicking the kernel's logic at a fraction of its computational cost. We apply our framework to Signal Temporal Logic (STL), demonstrating that the resulting neural representations faithfully preserve the semantic similarity of STL formulae, accurately predict robustness and constraint satisfaction, and remain intrinsically invertible. Our proposed approach enables highly efficient, scalable neuro-symbolic reasoning and formula reconstruction without repeated kernel computation at runtime.
title_zh: 将形式逻辑提炼至神经空间：一种用于信号时序逻辑的核对齐方法
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种框架，通过将形式化规范语义的几何结构提炼到潜在空间中，学习其连续的神经表示。现有方法要么依赖符号核函数——虽然能保持行为语义，但计算成本高、依赖锚点且不可逆；要么采用基于语法的神经嵌入，却无法捕捉底层结构。我们的方法填补了这一空白：通过教师-学生架构，我们将符号鲁棒性核函数提炼为一个Transformer编码器。与标准对比学习方法不同，我们采用一种连续的、基于核权重的几何对齐目标进行监督，惩罚程度与语义差异成正比。模型训练完成后，编码器仅需一次前向传播即可生成嵌入，以极低的计算开销有效模拟核函数的逻辑。我们将该框架应用于信号时序逻辑（STL），结果表明，所得神经表示能忠实保持STL公式的语义相似性，准确预测鲁棒性与约束满足情况，并且具有内在可逆性。所提出的方法实现了高效、可扩展的神经符号推理与公式重构，无需在运行时重复计算核函数。

title: RepoLaunch: Automating Build&Test Pipeline of Code Repositories on ANY Language and ANY Platform
authors: Kenan Li, Rongzhi Li, Linghao Zhang, Qirui Jin, Liao Zhu, Xiaosong Huang, Geng Zhang, Yikai Zhang, Shilin He, Chengxing Xie, Xin Zhang, Zijian Jin, Bowen Li, Chaoyun Zhang, Yu Kang, Yufan Huang, Elsie Nallipogu, Saravan Rajmohan, Qingwei Lin, Dongmei Zhang
abstract: Building software repositories typically requires significant manual effort. Recent advances in large language model (LLM) agents have accelerated automation in software engineering (SWE). We introduce RepoLaunch, the first agent capable of automatically resolving dependencies, compiling source code, and extracting test results for repositories across arbitrary programming languages and operating systems. To demonstrate its utility, we further propose a fully automated pipeline for SWE dataset creation, where task design is the only human intervention. RepoLaunch automates the remaining steps, enabling scalable benchmarking and training of coding agents and LLMs. Notably, several works on agentic benchmarking and training have recently adopted RepoLaunch for automated task generation.
title_zh: RepoLaunch：自动化任意语言和任意平台的代码仓库构建与测试流水线
abstract_zh: 构建软件仓库通常需要大量手动工作。近年来，大型语言模型（LLM）代理的进展加速了软件工程（SWE）的自动化进程。我们提出了RepoLaunch，这是首个能够自动解决依赖关系、编译源代码并提取测试结果的代理，适用于任意编程语言和操作系统下的仓库。为展示其实用性，我们进一步提出了一套完全自动化的SWE数据集创建流程，其中仅需人类参与任务设计。RepoLaunch自动完成其余步骤，从而实现了编码代理和LLM的可扩展基准测试与训练。值得注意的是，近期多项关于代理基准测试与训练的研究已采用RepoLaunch实现自动化任务生成。

title: When Weak LLMs Speak with Confidence, Preference Alignment Gets Stronger
authors: Amirabbas Afzali, Myeongho Jeon, Maria Brbic
abstract: Preference alignment is an essential step in adapting large language models (LLMs) to human values, but existing approaches typically depend on costly human annotations or large-scale API-based models. We explore whether a weak LLM can instead act as an effective annotator. We surprisingly find that selecting only a subset of a weak LLM's highly confident samples leads to substantially better performance than using full human annotations. Building on this insight, we propose Confidence-Weighted Preference Optimization (CW-PO), a general framework that re-weights training samples by a weak LLM's confidence and can be applied across different preference optimization objectives. Notably, the model aligned by CW-PO with just 20% of human annotations outperforms the model trained with 100% of annotations under standard DPO. These results suggest that weak LLMs, when paired with confidence weighting, can dramatically reduce the cost of preference alignment while even outperforming methods trained on fully human-labeled data.
title_zh: 当弱小的大型语言模型自信地表达时，偏好对齐会变得更强
abstract_zh: 偏好对齐是将大语言模型（LLMs）适配至人类价值观的关键步骤，但现有方法通常依赖于昂贵的人工标注或大规模基于API的模型。我们探索了是否可以使用一个较弱的LLM作为有效的标注工具。令人惊讶的是，我们发现仅选取弱LLM中高度自信的样本子集，其性能显著优于使用完整的人工标注数据。基于这一发现，我们提出了置信度加权偏好优化（CW-PO）框架，该框架通过弱LLM的置信度对训练样本进行重新加权，可适用于多种偏好优化目标。值得注意的是，仅使用20%人工标注数据并结合CW-PO进行对齐的模型，在标准DPO设置下，其性能超过了使用100%人工标注数据训练的模型。这些结果表明，当与置信度加权结合时，弱LLM能够大幅降低偏好对齐的成本，甚至超越完全依赖人工标注数据训练的方法。

title: $\nabla$-Reasoner: LLM Reasoning via Test-Time Gradient Descent in Latent Space
authors: Peihao Wang, Ruisi Cai, Zhen Wang, Hongyuan Mei, Qiang Liu, Pan Li, Zhangyang Wang
abstract:
Scaling inference-time compute for Large Language Models (LLMs) has unlocked unprecedented reasoning capabilities. However, existing inference-time scaling methods typically rely on inefficient and suboptimal discrete search algorithms or trial-and-error prompting to improve the online policy. In this paper, we propose $
abla$-Reasoner, an iterative generation framework that integrates differentiable optimization over token logits into the decoding loop to refine the policy on the fly. Our core component, Differentiable Textual Optimization (DTO), leverages gradient signals from both the LLM's likelihood and a reward model to refine textual representations. $
abla$-Reasoner further incorporates rejection sampling and acceleration design to robustify and speed up decoding. Theoretically, we show that performing inference-time gradient descent in the sample space to maximize reward is dual to aligning an LLM policy via KL-regularized reinforcement learning. Empirically, $
abla$-Reasoner achieves over 20% accuracy improvement on a challenging mathematical reasoning benchmark, while reducing number of model calls by approximately 10-40% compared to strong baselines. Overall, our work introduces a paradigm shift from zeroth-order search to first-order optimization at test time, offering a cost-effective path to amplify LLM reasoning.
title_zh: ∇-Reasoner：通过潜在空间中的测试时梯度下降实现大语言模型推理
abstract_zh:
在推理阶段扩展计算资源以提升大型语言模型（LLMs）的性能，已解锁了前所未有的推理能力。然而，现有的推理阶段扩展方法通常依赖于低效且次优的离散搜索算法或试错式提示（prompting）来优化在线策略。本文提出 $
abla$-Reasoner，一种迭代生成框架，将对 token logits 的可微优化整合到解码循环中，实现实时策略优化。我们的核心组件——可微文本优化（DTO），利用来自语言模型自身似然度以及奖励模型的梯度信号，对文本表示进行精细化调整。$
abla$-Reasoner 进一步结合拒绝采样（rejection sampling）与加速设计，以增强解码的鲁棒性并提升速度。理论上，我们证明了在样本空间中执行推理阶段的梯度下降以最大化奖励，等价于通过 KL 正则化的强化学习来对齐语言模型策略。实验结果表明，$
abla$-Reasoner 在一项具有挑战性的数学推理基准上实现了超过 20% 的准确率提升，同时相比强基线方法将模型调用次数减少了约 10%–40%。总体而言，我们的工作标志着从零阶搜索到一阶优化的范式转变，为提升 LLM 推理能力提供了一条成本效益极高的新路径。

title: Design Behaviour Codes (DBCs): A Taxonomy-Driven Layered Governance Benchmark for Large Language Models
authors: G. Madan Mohan, Veena Kiran Nambiar, Kiranmayee Janardhan
abstract: We introduce the Dynamic Behavioral Constraint (DBC) benchmark, the first empirical framework for evaluating the efficacy of a structured, 150-control behavioral governance layer, the MDBC (Madan DBC) system, applied at inference time to large language models (LLMs). Unlike training time alignment methods (RLHF, DPO) or post-hoc content moderation APIs, DBCs constitute a system prompt level governance layer that is model-agnostic, jurisdiction-mappable, and auditable. We evaluate the DBC Framework across a 30 domain risk taxonomy organized into six clusters (Hallucination and Calibration, Bias and Fairness, Malicious Use, Privacy and Data Protection, Robustness and Reliability, and Misalignment Agency) using an agentic red-team protocol with five adversarial attack strategies (Direct, Roleplay, Few-Shot, Hypothetical, Authority Spoof) across 3 model families. Our three-arm controlled design (Base, Base plus Moderation, Base plus DBC) enables causal attribution of risk reduction. Key findings: the DBC layer reduces the aggregate Risk Exposure Rate (RER) from 7.19 percent (Base) to 4.55 percent (Base plus DBC), representing a 36.8 percent relative risk reduction, compared with 0.6 percent for a standard safety moderation prompt. MDBC Adherence Scores improve from 8.6 by 10 (Base) to 8.7 by 10 (Base plus DBC). EU AI Act compliance (automated scoring) reaches 8.5by 10 under the DBC layer. A three judge evaluation ensemble yields Fleiss kappa greater than 0.70 (substantial agreement), validating our automated pipeline. Cluster ablation identifies the Integrity Protection cluster (MDBC 081 099) as delivering the highest per domain risk reduction, while graybox adversarial attacks achieve a DBC Bypass Rate of 4.83 percent . We release the benchmark code, prompt database, and all evaluation artefacts to enable reproducibility and longitudinal tracking as models evolve.
title_zh: 设计行为规范（DBCs）：一种基于分类学的分层治理基准，用于大型语言模型
abstract_zh: 我们提出了动态行为约束（Dynamic Behavioral Constraint, DBC）基准，这是首个用于评估结构化150项控制行为治理层在推理阶段应用效果的实证框架。该治理层即MDBC（Madan DBC）系统，专为大型语言模型（LLMs）设计。与训练阶段对齐方法（如RLHF、DPO）或事后内容审核API不同，DBC构成一个模型无关、可映射至司法辖区、且可审计的系统级提示治理层。我们采用一个涵盖30个领域风险的分类体系（分为六大集群：幻觉与校准、偏见与公平性、恶意使用、隐私与数据保护、鲁棒性与可靠性、错位代理），通过五种对抗性攻击策略（直接攻击、角色扮演、少样本提示、假设性情境、权威伪装）对三个模型家族进行评估，使用代理红队协议进行测试。我们的三臂对照实验设计（基础模型、基础模型+内容审核、基础模型+DBC）实现了对风险降低的因果归因。主要发现如下：DBC层将综合风险暴露率（RER）从基础模型的7.19%降低至4.55%，相对风险降低达36.8%，而标准安全审核提示仅实现0.6%的降低。MDBC遵从度评分从基础模型的8.6/10提升至8.7/10（基础模型+DBC）。在DBC层下，欧盟《人工智能法案》合规性（自动化评分）达到8.5/10。由三位评审员组成的评估集合得出Fleiss Kappa值大于0.70（显著一致性），验证了我们自动化评估流程的有效性。集群消融分析表明，“完整性保护”集群（MDBC 081 099）在各领域中实现了最高的风险降低效果，而灰盒对抗攻击的DBC绕过率仅为4.83%。我们已公开发布该基准的代码、提示数据库及所有评估成果，以支持可复现性，并实现对模型演进过程的长期追踪。

title: Beyond Linear LLM Invocation: An Efficient and Effective Semantic Filter Paradigm
authors: Nan Hou, Kangfei Zhao, Jiadong Xie, Jeffrey Xu Yu
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for semantic query processing over large corpora. A set of semantic operators derived from relational algebra has been proposed to provide a unified interface for expressing such queries, among which the semantic filter operator serves as a cornerstone. Given a table T with a natural language predicate e, for each tuple in the relation, the execution of a semantic filter proceeds by constructing an input prompt that combines the predicate e with its content, querying the LLM, and obtaining the binary decision. However, this tuple-by-tuple evaluation necessitates a complete linear scan of the table, incurring prohibitive latency and token costs. Although recent work has attempted to optimize semantic filtering, it still does not break the linear LLM invocation barriers. To address this, we propose Clustering-Sampling-Voting (CSV), a new framework that reduces LLM invocations to sublinear complexity while providing error guarantees. CSV embeds tuples into semantic clusters, samples a small subset for LLM evaluation, and infers cluster-level labels via two proposed voting strategies: UniVote, which aggregates labels uniformly, and SimVote, which weights votes by semantic similarity. Moreover, CSV triggers re-clustering on ambiguous clusters to ensure robustness across diverse datasets. The results conducted on real-world datasets demonstrate that CSV reduces the number of LLM calls by 1.28-355x compared to the state-of-the-art approaches, while maintaining comparable effectiveness in terms of Accuracy and F1 score.
title_zh: 超越线性大语言模型调用：一种高效且有效的语义过滤范式
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在大规模语料库的语义查询处理中正得到越来越广泛的应用。为统一表达此类查询，已有研究提出了一组源自关系代数的语义操作符，其中语义过滤操作符作为核心组件。给定一张表T和一个自然语言谓词e，语义过滤的执行过程是：对关系中的每个元组，将谓词e与其内容结合构造输入提示，调用LLM进行查询，并获取二元判断结果。然而，这种逐元组评估方式需要对表进行完整的线性扫描，导致显著的延迟和高昂的令牌消耗。尽管近期工作尝试优化语义过滤，但仍未能突破线性调用LLM的瓶颈。为此，我们提出Clustering-Sampling-Voting（CSV）框架，该框架将LLM调用次数降至亚线性复杂度，同时提供误差保证。CSV首先将元组嵌入到语义聚类中，然后对少量子集进行LLM评估，并通过两种新提出的投票策略推断聚类级别的标签：UniVote采用均匀聚合标签，SimVote则根据语义相似度对投票进行加权。此外，CSV在识别出模糊聚类时会触发重新聚类，以确保在多样化数据集上的鲁棒性。在真实世界数据集上的实验结果表明，与现有最先进方法相比，CSV将LLM调用次数减少了1.28至355倍，同时在准确率（Accuracy）和F1分数方面保持了相当的性能。

title: Behaviour Driven Development Scenario Generation with Large Language Models
authors: Amila Rathnayake, Mojtaba Shahin, Golnoush Abaei
abstract: This paper presents an evaluation of three LLMs, GPT-4, Claude 3, and Gemini, for automated Behaviour-Driven Development (BDD) scenarios generation. To support this evaluation, we constructed a dataset of 500 user stories, requirement descriptions, and their corresponding BDD scenarios, drawn from four proprietary software products. We assessed the quality of BDD scenarios generated by LLMs using a multidimensional evaluation framework encompassing text and semantic similarity metrics, LLM-based evaluation, and human expert assessment. Our findings reveal that although GPT-4 achieves higher scores in text and semantic similarity metrics, Claude 3 produces scenarios rated highest by both human experts and LLM-based evaluators. LLM-based evaluators, particularly DeepSeek, show a stronger correlation with human judgment than with text similarity and semantic similarity metrics. The effectiveness of prompting techniques is model-specific: GPT-4 performs best with zero-shot, Claude 3 benefits from chain-of-thought reasoning, and Gemini achieves optimal results with few-shot examples. Input quality determines the effectiveness of BDD scenario generation: detailed requirement descriptions alone yield high-quality scenarios, whereas user stories alone yield low-quality scenarios. Our experiments indicate that setting temperature to 0 and top_p to 1.0 produced the highest-quality BDD scenarios across all models.
title_zh: 基于大语言模型的行为驱动开发场景生成
abstract_zh: 本文对三种大语言模型（GPT-4、Claude 3 和 Gemini）在自动化行为驱动开发（BDD）场景生成中的表现进行了评估。为支持该评估，我们构建了一个包含500个用户故事、需求描述及其对应BDD场景的数据集，数据来源于四个专有软件产品。我们采用多维度评估框架来衡量LLM生成的BDD场景质量，该框架涵盖文本与语义相似性指标、基于LLM的评估以及人工专家评审。研究结果表明，尽管GPT-4在文本和语义相似性指标上得分更高，但Claude 3生成的场景在人工专家和基于LLM的评估者中均获得最高评价。基于LLM的评估者（尤其是DeepSeek）与人类判断的相关性高于与文本或语义相似性指标的相关性。提示技术的有效性具有模型特异性：GPT-4在零样本提示下表现最佳，Claude 3受益于思维链（chain-of-thought）推理，而Gemini则在少量示例提示下达到最优效果。输入质量决定了BDD场景生成的效果：仅提供详细的需求描述即可生成高质量场景，而仅依赖用户故事则导致低质量输出。实验还表明，将温度参数设为0、top_p设为1.0，可在所有模型中生成最高质量的BDD场景。

title: Judge Reliability Harness: Stress Testing the Reliability of LLM Judges
authors: Sunishchal Dev, Andrew Sloan, Joshua Kavner, Nicholas Kong, Morgan Sandler
abstract: We present the Judge Reliability Harness, an open source library for constructing validation suites that test the reliability of LLM judges. As LLM based scoring is widely deployed in AI benchmarks, more tooling is needed to efficiently assess the reliability of these methods. Given a benchmark dataset and an LLM judge configuration, the harness generates reliability tests that evaluate both binary judgment accuracy and ordinal grading performance for free-response and agentic task formats. We evaluate four state-of-the-art judges across four benchmarks spanning safety, persuasion, misuse, and agentic behavior, and find meaningful variation in performance across models and perturbation types, highlighting opportunities to improve the robustness of LLM judges. No judge that we evaluated is uniformly reliable across benchmarks using our harness. For example, our preliminary experiments on judges revealed consistency issues as measured by accuracy in judging another LLM's ability to complete a task due to simple text formatting changes, paraphrasing, changes in verbosity, and flipping the ground truth label in LLM-produced responses. The code for this tool is available at: https://github.com/RANDCorporation/judge-reliability-harness
title_zh: 法官可靠性测试仪：对大语言模型法官的可靠性进行压力测试
abstract_zh: 我们提出了“裁判可靠性测试框架”（Judge Reliability Harness），这是一个开源库，用于构建验证套件，以测试大语言模型（LLM）裁判的可靠性。随着基于LLM的评分方法在人工智能基准测试中被广泛采用，亟需更多工具来高效评估这些方法的可靠性。给定一个基准数据集和LLM裁判配置，该框架可自动生成可靠性测试，免费评估二元判断准确率以及自由回答和代理型任务格式下的序数评分表现。我们在涵盖安全、说服力、滥用行为和代理行为四个领域的四个基准上，评估了四种最先进的裁判模型，发现不同模型及扰动类型之间存在显著的性能差异，凸显了提升LLM裁判鲁棒性的潜在空间。我们评估的所有裁判在使用该框架时均未表现出在所有基准上均一致可靠的特性。例如，初步实验发现，仅因文本格式的简单变化、改写、冗长度调整，或在LLM生成的回答中翻转真实标签，就会导致裁判在判断另一LLM完成任务能力时出现一致性问题。该工具的代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/RANDCorporation/judge-reliability-harness

title: DARE: Aligning LLM Agents with the R Statistical Ecosystem via Distribution-Aware Retrieval
authors: Maojun Sun, Yue Wu, Yifei Xie, Ruijian Han, Binyan Jiang, Defeng Sun, Yancheng Yuan, Jian Huang
abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) agents can automate data-science workflows, but many rigorous statistical methods implemented in R remain underused because LLMs struggle with statistical knowledge and tool retrieval. Existing retrieval-augmented approaches focus on function-level semantics and ignore data distribution, producing suboptimal matches. We propose DARE (Distribution-Aware Retrieval Embedding), a lightweight, plug-and-play retrieval model that incorporates data distribution information into function representations for R package retrieval. Our main contributions are: (i) RPKB, a curated R Package Knowledge Base derived from 8,191 high-quality CRAN packages; (ii) DARE, an embedding model that fuses distributional features with function metadata to improve retrieval relevance; and (iii) RCodingAgent, an R-oriented LLM agent for reliable R code generation and a suite of statistical analysis tasks for systematically evaluating LLM agents in realistic analytical scenarios. Empirically, DARE achieves an NDCG at 10 of 93.47%, outperforming state-of-the-art open-source embedding models by up to 17% on package retrieval while using substantially fewer parameters. Integrating DARE into RCodingAgent yields significant gains on downstream analysis tasks. This work helps narrow the gap between LLM automation and the mature R statistical ecosystem.
title_zh: DARE：通过分布感知检索实现大语言模型代理与R统计生态系统的对齐
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）代理能够自动化数据科学工作流程，但许多在R语言中实现的严谨统计方法仍因LLM在统计知识和工具检索方面存在困难而未被充分利用。现有的检索增强方法主要关注函数级别的语义信息，却忽略了数据分布特征，导致匹配效果不佳。为此，我们提出DARE（Distribution-Aware Retrieval Embedding），一种轻量级、即插即用的检索模型，通过将数据分布信息融入函数表示，以提升R包检索的准确性。本文的主要贡献包括：（i）RPKB，一个基于8,191个高质量CRAN包构建的精心整理的R包知识库；（ii）DARE，一种融合分布特征与函数元数据的嵌入模型，显著提升了检索的相关性；（iii）RCodingAgent，一个面向R语言的LLM代理，可生成可靠R代码，并支持一系列统计分析任务，用于在真实分析场景下系统评估LLM代理的表现。实验结果表明，DARE在包检索任务上的NDCG@10达到93.47%，相比当前最先进的开源嵌入模型最高提升17%，同时使用参数量大幅减少。将DARE集成到RCodingAgent中后，在下游分析任务上取得了显著性能提升。本研究有助于缩小LLM自动化与成熟的R统计生态之间的差距。

title: Transducing Language Models
authors: Vésteinn Snæbjarnarson, Samuel Kiegeland, Tianyu Liu, Reda Boumasmoud, Ryan Cotterell, Tim Vieira
abstract: Modern language models define distributions over strings, but downstream tasks often require different output formats. For instance, a model that generates byte-pair strings does not directly produce word-level predictions, and a DNA model does not directly produce amino-acid sequences. In such cases, a deterministic string-to-string transformation can convert the model's output to the desired form. This is a familiar pattern in probability theory: applying a function $f$ to a random variable $X\sim p$ yields a transformed random variable $f(X)$ with an induced distribution. While such transformations are occasionally used in language modeling, prior work does not treat them as yielding new, fully functional language models. We formalize this perspective and introduce a general framework for language models derived from deterministic string-to-string transformations. We focus on transformations representable as finite-state transducers -- a commonly used state-machine abstraction for efficient string-to-string mappings. We develop algorithms that compose a language model with an FST to *marginalize* over source strings mapping to a given target, propagating probabilities through the transducer without altering model parameters and enabling *conditioning* on transformed outputs. We present an exact algorithm, an efficient approximation, and a theoretical analysis. We conduct experiments in three domains: converting language models from tokens to bytes, from tokens to words, and from DNA to amino acids. These experiments demonstrate inference-time adaptation of pretrained language models to match application-specific output requirements.
title_zh: 转换语言模型
abstract_zh: 现代语言模型定义的是字符串上的概率分布，但下游任务通常需要不同的输出格式。例如，生成字节对字符串的模型并不能直接产生词级预测，而DNA模型也无法直接生成氨基酸序列。在这种情况下，可以通过确定性的字符串到字符串的转换，将模型的输出转换为所需的形式。这在概率论中是一个常见模式：对服从分布 $ p $ 的随机变量 $ X $ 应用函数 $ f $，会得到一个变换后的随机变量 $ f(X) $，其具有由原分布诱导出的新分布。尽管这类变换在语言建模中偶尔被使用，但以往的研究并未将其视为生成全新、完整可用的语言模型。本文正式提出了这一视角，并引入了一个基于确定性字符串到字符串变换的语言模型通用框架。我们重点关注可由有限状态转换器（FST）表示的变换——这是一种广泛用于高效字符串映射的有限状态机抽象。我们开发了算法，将语言模型与FST进行组合，以对映射到特定目标字符串的所有源字符串进行*边缘化*，在不改变模型参数的前提下，将概率通过转换器传播，并实现对变换后输出的*条件化*。本文提出了一个精确算法、一种高效的近似算法以及相应的理论分析。我们在三个领域进行了实验：将基于词元的语言模型转换为字节表示、转换为词级表示，以及将DNA序列转换为氨基酸序列。这些实验展示了在推理阶段对预训练语言模型进行适应，以满足特定应用的输出需求。

title: Application of Deep Reinforcement Learning in FPGA Multi-Frequency Clock Phase-Shifting Adaptive Measurement
authors: Zhao Yulong, Wang Jianxiang, Wang Qian, Wang Dongjun, Cai Zhiwei, Wang Kuan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This paper, in view of traditional FPGA clock frequency phase shift in a dynamic environment adaptive measuring method, precision limited problem, this study proposes a reinforcement learning based on depth (DRL) adaptive measurement algorithm. By constructing a decision-making framework for the interaction between the agent and the environment, a multi-dimensional state space is designed, which includes clock frequency characteristics, phase deviation indicators, environmental interference parameters and system resource states, and a discrete-continuous hybrid action space is determined, such as phase adjustment and measurement strategy selection. As the core, combined with the phase deviation both reward function, resource consumption and environmental interference ResNet and attention mechanism of hybrid neural network is adopted to improve the end-to-end optimization. The experimental results show that compared with traditional method, the DRL will measure precision up to 30 ps, precision is 47%. At the same time, through the model pruning and knowledge distillation technology, the computing resource consumption is reduced by 63%. The algorithm in dynamic change frequency clock jitter and + 15 ps environment conditions show the strong robustness, precision measuring equipment for high performance computing and provides a high precision, low utilization of adaptive measuring solution.
title_zh: 深度强化学习在FPGA多频钟相位移自适应测量中的应用
abstract_zh: 针对传统FPGA时钟频率相位偏移在动态环境下自适应测量方法精度受限的问题，本文提出了一种基于深度强化学习（DRL）的自适应测量算法。通过构建智能体与环境之间的交互决策框架，设计了包含时钟频率特性、相位偏差指标、环境干扰参数及系统资源状态的多维状态空间，并确定了离散-连续混合动作空间，如相位调节与测量策略选择。作为核心部分，结合相位偏差、资源消耗及环境干扰的综合奖励函数，采用ResNet与注意力机制融合的混合神经网络，以实现端到端的优化。实验结果表明，与传统方法相比，该DRL算法将测量精度提升至30 ps，精度提高47%。同时，通过模型剪枝与知识蒸馏技术，计算资源消耗降低63%。该算法在动态频率变化的时钟抖动及+15 ps环境条件下均表现出强鲁棒性，为高性能计算领域提供了高精度、低资源占用的自适应测量解决方案。

title: Design and Application of an Evaluation System for Large Language Models
authors: Ying Shen, Zhiyuan Cao, Mingang Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of Large Language Models (LLMs) across various domains, establishing an evaluation system has become increasingly critical. However, existing mainstream evaluation methodologies still suffer from limitations in both evaluation datasets and evaluation methods. To address these limitations, we propose an innovative evaluation system for LLMs. The system comprises four core components: (1) definition of evaluation dimensions, (2) construction of evaluation datasets, (3) automated interaction, and (4) automated evaluation, together forming a complete closed-loop evaluation pipeline. Notably, to bridge the gap between existing evaluation benchmarks and the latest standards, we construct a multi-dimensional evaluation dataset fully aligned with those standards. Based on this dataset, we perform a comprehensive evaluation of multiple LLMs, systematically revealing their performance across dimensions defined by standards.
title_zh: 大型语言模型评估体系的设计与应用
abstract_zh: 随着大型语言模型（LLMs）在各个领域的广泛应用，建立一套有效的评估体系变得日益重要。然而，现有的主流评估方法在评估数据集和评估手段方面仍存在诸多局限。为解决这些问题，我们提出了一种创新的LLM评估系统。该系统包含四个核心组成部分：（1）评估维度的定义，（2）评估数据集的构建，（3）自动化交互，以及（4）自动化评估，共同构成一个完整的闭环评估流程。特别地，为了弥合现有评估基准与最新标准之间的差距，我们构建了一个全面符合最新标准的多维度评估数据集。基于该数据集，我们对多个LLM进行了全面评估，系统性地揭示了它们在标准所定义各维度上的表现。

title: Evaluating Functional Coverage of Accelerator Design for DT-based ML Implementations
authors: Ayush Dixit, Raushan Kumar, Abhishek Yadav, Binod Kumar
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: This work explores the functional coverage assessment of Decision Tree(DT)-based classification accelerator designs. Our investigation demonstrates that decision tree algorithms can be efficiently implemented in hardware with minimal resource utilization while maintaining high classification accuracy. The proposed accelerator design achieves 96.66% accuracy on the benchmark Iris dataset using Python libraries (Pandas, Scikit-learn, and Matplotlib) while consuming only 0.96 W of power in Vivado 2022.1 and utilizing just 0.07% of available look-up tables (LUTs) and 0.01% of flip-flops (FFs) on the Basys3 Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) platform. Comprehensive functional coverage analysis using the Synopsys VCS tool confirms 100% coverage across all variables by incorporating a new reference model for coverage collection in addition to the conventional universal verification methodology (UVM) testbench architecture, ensuring robust verification. The findings highlight the potential of decision tree-based accelerators for resource-constrained applications where power and area constraints are critical. Codes and subsequent files are available at GitHub Repository.
title_zh: 基于DT的机器学习实现中加速器设计的功能覆盖率评估
abstract_zh: 本文探讨了基于决策树（DT）分类加速器设计的功能覆盖率评估。我们的研究结果表明，决策树算法能够以极低的资源开销在硬件上高效实现，同时保持较高的分类准确率。所提出的加速器设计在基准数据集Iris上使用Python库（Pandas、Scikit-learn和Matplotlib）实现了96.66%的准确率，而在Vivado 2022.1环境下仅消耗0.96 W的功耗，并且在Basys3现场可编程门阵列（FPGA）平台上仅占用0.07%的查找表（LUTs）和0.01%的触发器（FFs）。通过使用Synopsys VCS工具进行全面的功能覆盖率分析，结合一种新的参考模型用于覆盖率收集，以及传统的通用验证方法学（UVM）测试平台架构，验证了所有变量均达到100%的覆盖率，确保了验证的可靠性。研究结果表明，基于决策树的加速器在资源受限的应用中具有巨大潜力，尤其适用于对功耗和面积有严格要求的场景。代码及相关文件可在GitHub仓库中获取。

title: A Fault-Tolerant Die-to-Die Interconnect with Retry and Repair Mechanisms for Chiplet-Based Systems
authors: Yan Qiao, Bi Wang, Shuai Hao, Wei Zhuang, Yong Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: With the slowdown of Moore's Law, chiplet-based integration has emerged as a promising strategy for improving system performance and yield in the post-Moore era. The die-todie (D2D) interconnect serves as the critical interface enabling communication among chiplets. However, the use of dense microbump arrays and advanced packaging technologies increase the risk of contact failures, thereby posing significant challenges to system reliability. This paper presents a lightweight and faulttolerant D2D interconnect that integrates a retry mechanism with a link repair scheme to ensure data integrity and maintain system availability. Implemented in Verilog and validated on a Xilinx XCVU440 FPGA, the proposed D2D interconnect achieves 14.76 Gb/s throughput and a 37.5% reduction in hardware resource utilization compared with a previously reported low-cost chip-to-chip interconnect.
title_zh: 一种具有重试与修复机制的容错型芯粒间互连结构用于芯粒系统
abstract_zh: 随着摩尔定律的放缓，基于芯粒（chiplet）的集成技术已成为后摩尔时代提升系统性能与良率的一种有前景的策略。die-to-die（D2D）互连作为实现芯粒间通信的关键接口，其重要性日益凸显。然而，密集的微凸块阵列和先进的封装技术也带来了接触失效风险的增加，对系统可靠性构成了严峻挑战。本文提出了一种轻量级且具备容错能力的D2D互连方案，通过集成重试机制与链路修复策略，有效保障数据完整性并维持系统可用性。该方案采用Verilog实现，并在Xilinx XCVU440 FPGA平台上完成验证，相比此前报道的一种低成本芯粒间互连，实现了14.76 Gb/s的吞吐量，同时硬件资源利用率降低了37.5%。

title: Reliable AI Accelerator Design Using Multiple Fault-Tolerance Techniques on SRAM-Based APSoCs
authors: Eduardo Marañon, Fabio Benevenuti, Nilberto H. Medina, Nemitala Added, Vitor Ângelo P. Aguiar, Saulo G. P. N. Alberton, João Vitor Schervenin, Matheus S. Pereira, Fernanda L. Kastensmidt
abstract: As Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) implemented on All Programmable System on Chips (APSoCs) are increasingly adopted in space mission-critical systems due to their flexibility and parallelism, selecting a fault-tolerant design that balances reliability, area, and power is essential to address radiation-induced faults and resource constraints. However, the literature offers limited exploration of the combined use of multiple fault-tolerance techniques within a single AI accelerator, making it difficult to identify the most effective strategies. To address this, we propose a generalizable design flow and apply it to the ZynqNet accelerator which is generated using High-Level Synthesis (HLS). ZynqNet implements a 4-layer CNN trained on the SAT-6 dataset for aerial image classification. The goal is to evaluate the impact of several fault-tolerance strategies applied at different levels of architectural granularity based on Triple Modular Redundancy (TMR), including Modular TMR (MTMR), Coarse-Grained TMR (CGTMR), and Distributed TMR (DTMR), targeting bit flips in the configuration memory (CRAM) of the Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA).
